children.heading: .—Program Required
children.label: a .—Program Required
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall establish a program for information sharing protection and insider threat mitigation for the information systems of the Department of Defense to detect unauthorized access to, use of, or transmission of classified or controlled unclassified information.

children.label: A
children.text: monitoring the use of external ports and read and write capability controls;

children.label: B
children.text: disabling the removable media ports of computers physically or electronically;

children.label: C
children.text: electronic auditing and reporting of unusual and unauthorized user activities;

children.label: D
children.text: using data-loss prevention and data-rights management technology to prevent the unauthorized export of information from a network or to render such information unusable in the event of the unauthorized export of such information;

children.label: F
children.text: cross-domain guards for transfers of information between different networks; and

children.label: G
children.text: patch management for software and security updates.

Policies and procedures to support such program, including special consideration for policies and procedures related to international and interagency partners and activities in support of ongoing operations in areas of hostilities.

children.label: A
children.text: coordination with the existing security clearance and suitability review process;

children.label: B
children.text: coordination of existing anomaly detection techniques, including those used in counterintelligence investigation or personnel screening activities; and

children.label: C
children.text: updating and expediting of the classification review and marking process.

children.label: A
children.text: gaps in security measures under the program; and

children.label: B
children.text: technology, policies, and processes needed to increase the capability of the program beyond the initially established full operating capability to address such gaps.
A baseline analysis framework that includes measures of performance and effectiveness.
A plan for how to ensure related security measures are put in place for other departments or agencies with access to Department of Defense networks.
A plan for enforcement to ensure that the program is being applied and implemented on a uniform and consistent basis.
